page: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor architecture operating at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server includes 1890 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths placing in the top 10% tier. Passmark memory scores and database operations also rank in the top 10% tier, while multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the top 25% tier. However, single-core performance benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, remain in the middle 50% average tier. Large-scale language model inference shows a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a 70B model scoring in the bottom 10% tier, though text generation for the same model recovers to the middle 50% average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density of 8.0 GB per core combined with the power-efficient ARM architecture provides a favorable resource-to-overhead ratio for memory-intensive workloads. The presence of 1890 GB of local storage, paired with top-tier database operation benchmarks and strong Redis SET performance, makes this server highly suitable for relational databases, in-memory caching layers, and high-throughput web serving. Conversely, the lack of AVX instruction support and poor large-model LLM prompt processing speeds make it less suitable for heavy vector-based machine learning or high-performance computing workloads that rely on x86-specific instruction sets.
description: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. It is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, offering a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, alongside 1890 GB of local storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in memory bandwidth and database operations, alongside strong multi-core processing and Redis SET throughput. However, single-core execution and large-model LLM prompt processing represent performance tradeoffs, with the latter falling into the bottom 10% tier. This server provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads due to its high resource density and ARM architecture. It is best suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and static web serving, while being less optimal for vector-heavy compute tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32pds_v6 is an ARM-based VM with 32 cores, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, built for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine combining high memory density and local storage for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM cores operating at 3.4 GHz
256.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory density
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark results
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and web serving
Performance tradeoffs in single-core tasks and large-model LLM prompt processing
